system.description: Ice of the Heart: This disease is caused by an excess of cold in the heart. It is most commonly the result of the northern feast beetle, which is parasitic and burrows into the human body until it reaches the heart. Once there, it waits until the person consumes water. As soon as water is consumed the beetle draws it into the heart and releases a terrible poison throughout the system that causes the body to literally freeze. When this occurs the person's limbs become stiff like ice and they die within minutes. The beetle then consumes the frozen flesh slowly over the course of months.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 2: Rules
